message:
fix(runtime): Add comparison_ops for O(1) monomorphization reduction

Problem: anytype patterns in operator.zig and assert_equal.zig caused
O(n²) monomorphization explosion when called with different type pairs.
This was a root cause of Core2 compilation timeouts.

Solution: PyValue dispatch pattern - concrete comparison functions that
compile once instead of per type-pair:

- Create comparison_ops.zig with concrete functions:
  - eqI64/ltI64/etc. for i64 comparisons
  - eqF64/ltF64/etc. for f64 comparisons
  - eqStr/ltStr/etc. for string comparisons
  - eqPyValue/ltPyValue/etc. for PyValue comparisons
  - Cross-type: eqI64F64/eqF64I64/etc.

- Update operator.zig to dispatch common types to comparison_ops
  instead of direct anytype comparisons

- Add fast paths in assert_equal.zig for common types (PyValue, i64,
  f64, bool, string) that return early without complex type introspection

Expected impact:
- operator.eq/lt/etc.: O(n²) type pairs → O(1) concrete + dispatch
- assertEqual: O(n²) × 728 lines → fast paths + fallback
- Core2 batch compile: should complete without timeout
